description: Recto, bottom: accounts in Judaeo-Arabic and Hebrew numerals. This is unrelated to the list of Sabbath desecrators except that it seems to be in the hand of the same scribe. The entries list various items of furniture and household goods, their values, and people associated with them. The last four entries all have the word "nafadha" above them ("it was carried out"). The names include al-Dayyan; Ṭāhir; and Mufaḍḍal (Ṣayrafī?). The items include tables (מאידה), chairs (כרסי), steps (? דרג), a chest (צנדוק), a knife, a small table, and an engraved piece of wood (khashab manqūsh)
description_he: רשימה או טבלה ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
